pageIntro: Quick reference for the verb and vocabulary study pages. Focus on high-frequency present, past, and future patterns you can reuse right away.

sections.title: 1) Present tense basics
sections.intro: Start with short, useful sentences in the present tense. Hungarian often allows dropping the pronoun because the verb ending already shows who is doing the action.
points: Practical beginner order: Subject + Object + Verb (for example: Én vizet iszom).
Pronouns are optional in many sentences: Iszom vizet also means I drink water.
One verb form changes for each person (I, you, he/she, etc.).
Learn frequent verbs first: lenni (to be), menni (to go), enni (to eat), inni (to drink), tanulni (to study).

tables.title: Present tense snapshot (verbs already in this app)

sections.title: 2) Pronouns you will see most
sections.intro: These pronouns match the person endings in verb tables.
points: Use pronouns for clarity or emphasis. In normal conversation they are often omitted.
Ő can mean he or she. Hungarian does not force a gender distinction here.
Magyar often sounds more natural with fewer explicit pronouns than English.

sections.title: 3) Common conjugation patterns (present tense)
sections.intro: You do not need every exception at once. These patterns cover many beginner verbs.
points: Many regular-style verbs use endings like -ok/-ek (én), -sz (te), no ending (ő), -unk/-ünk (mi), -tok/-tek (ti), -nak/-nek (ők).
Vowel harmony affects which vowel appears in endings (for example, -ok vs -ek, -unk vs -ünk).
Some high-frequency verbs are irregular and must be memorized (especially lenni and parts of menni).
Some verbs show an -ik form in third person singular (for example: eszik, iszik).

sections.title: 4) Past tense basics
sections.intro: For beginner conversation, one core past form covers all persons. Person endings are added to that past stem.
points: A common pattern is past stem + personal ending (for example: tanult-am, tanult-ál, tanult).
Many useful verbs in this app show a clear past form: mentem, ettem, ittam, tanultam.
Use time words to make meaning clearer: tegnap (yesterday), ma reggel (this morning).

examples.breakdown: tegnap = yesterday, iskolába = to school, mentem = I went

sections.title: 5) Future tense basics
sections.intro: A beginner-friendly way to form future is infinitive + fog + person ending. You can use this with many verbs quickly.
points: Examples from app verbs: menni fogok, tanulni fogunk, inni fogsz.
Use time words for context: holnap (tomorrow), később (later).

examples.breakdown: holnap = tomorrow, magyarul = in Hungarian, fogok tanulni = I will study
kenyeret = bread (object), fog enni = will eat

sections.title: 6) Worked examples (step by step)
sections.intro: Use these as mini-templates when you build your own sentences.

examples.breakdown: iskolába = to school, megy = he/she goes
magyarul = in Hungarian, tanulunk = we study
